draft.introduction: Lamentations 3 is the central and longest poem of the five laments over Jerusalem’s destruction. At the nadir of suffering, it pivots to the declaration that God’s mercies “are new every morning.”

translation.text: The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases; his mercies never come to an end; they are new every morning; great is your faithfulness. ‘The Lord is my portion,’ says my soul, ‘therefore I will hope in him.’ The Lord is good to those who wait for him, to the soul who seeks him. It is good that one should wait quietly for the salvation of the Lord.
translation.original_text: ἐλέη Κυρίου ὅτι οὐκ ἐξέλιπον ὅτι οὐκ ἐτελειώθησαν οἱ οἰκτιρμοὶ αὐτοῦ

translator_notes.note: “Great is your faithfulness” emerges not from prosperity but from the ashes of Jerusalem’s destruction. The theological weight of this affirmation depends entirely on its context of devastation—faith spoken against all evidence.
